title: rubus koehleri var. omalodontus
intro: Rubus koehleri var. omalodontus, also known as the Japanese raspberry or Japanese bramble, is a variety of bramble shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus koehleri var. omalodontus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Rubus koehleri var. omalodontus is typically prepared as a decoction or tea, and may also be used in the form of extracts or capsules in modern herbal preparations.
